topic.what: End-to-end privacy-preserving RAG enabling secure retrieval over encrypted knowledge bases without sacrificing scalability
topic.how: Dual-mode CKKS-based homomorphic encryption system with encrypted HNSW index, Chebyshev polynomial ranking approximation, and client-assisted exact comparison modes
topic.why_matters: Closes critical gap between privacy-preserving RAG and practical cloud deployment by achieving end-to-end confidentiality for documents and queries while maintaining retrieval quality and scalability

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of relevant documents retrieved in top-k results
characteristics.evidence: Experimental evaluation on large-scale datasets in Section VI-B
characteristics.context: PRAG-I achieves 72.45% recall; PRAG-II achieves 74.45% recall

characteristics.what_it_measures: End-to-end retrieval time for top-10 queries
characteristics.context: PRAG-I is non-interactive; PRAG-II requires multiple client-server rounds

characteristics.vs_baseline: Exceeds DP-based methods that protect only queries or documents
characteristics.evidence: Theorems VI.5-VI.7 provide formal IND-CPA-based proofs
characteristics.context: Full end-to-end protection vs partial solutions in Table I

characteristics.vs_baseline: Previous SSR schemes vulnerable to LAA
characteristics.context: Achieved via dummy traversal injection and periodic re-encryption

characteristics.what_it_measures: Preservation of relative document ordering under homomorphic noise
characteristics.vs_baseline: Standard MSE metrics insufficient for ranking quality
characteristics.evidence: OEE model in Section V-C bounds ranking distortion probability
characteristics.context: Novel metric targeting ranking preservation rather than numeric accuracy
Scalability to concurrent queries not evaluated

good_for: sensitive document retrieval in regulated industries
multi-user collaborative RAG with data confidentiality
defense against cloud provider data breaches
not_for: Low-power client devices unable to handle decryption overhead
Settings requiring plaintext corpus for admin inspection
Pure local deployment scenarios without cloud benefits
requires: Sufficient computational resources for HE operations
Periodic re-encryption capability for access pattern mitigation

novelty: End-to-end confidentiality for both documents and queries in RAG
Dual-mode architecture enabling latency-accuracy trade-off without re-encryption
Operation-Error Estimation (OEE) for noise-aware ranking stabilization
Formal access-pattern mitigation against graph reconstruction attacks
open_problems: Reducing homomorphic computation overhead for larger datasets
Improving semantic query decomposition success rate
Scaling access pattern mitigation to higher query volumes
Reducing client interaction rounds in PRAG-II
